headlines.title: What Your Skin Could Be Telling You About Your Health
headlines.description: A lot of people have a skin care routine but did you know your skin could actually be sounding the alarm about issues happening inside your body, including dementia and diabetes? Dr. Saranya Wyles, a board-certified dermatologist and physician-scientist at the Mayo Clinic, joins TODAY with tips on how to decode those warning signs.

headlines.title: Inside Look at Concerns Around Weight Loss Drug Overdoses
headlines.description: As the popularity of GLP-1 drugs continues to skyrocket, there has also been a huge rise in overdoses. Dozens of online telehealth platforms have made these medications incredibly easy to obtain but often without proper supervision to ensure the medications are being taken safely. In partnership with KFF Health News, NBC’s Anne Thompson reports for TODAY.

headlines.title: Tips to Stay Safe as Cases of Major Stomach Bug Grow Across US
headlines.description: The CDC has confirmed more than 1,600 cyclosporiasis cases, the nasty food-borne parasite that causes severe stomach issues and has spread across 34 states. NBC’s Vicky Nguyen joins TODAY with tips on how to stay safe amid the outbreak, including what foods to eat and which to avoid.

headlines.title: Officials Warn Stomach Bug Outbreak Could Last Through Summer
headlines.description: The stomach-churning cyclosporiasis outbreak has now spread to 34 states and the CDC is anticipating cases will continue to grow. Although federal health officials have not yet confirmed a specific food item as the source of the outbreak, lettuce and other leafy greens are being eyed as the potential culprit. NBC’s Anne Thompson reports for TODAY.

headlines.title: Hair oil and scalp oil are not the same thing — here’s what experts want you to know
headlines.description: Hair oil and scalp oil aren’t interchangeable. Hairstylists and trichologists explain the difference, how to use each one and their top product picks.

headlines.title: Is Thirdlove’s cooling bra actually cooling? I tested it for over a year to find out
headlines.description: I tested the Thirdlove TempSync T-Shirt Bra for a year to see if it’s actually cooling, quick-drying and helps prevent boob sweat. Here are my honest thoughts, plus sizing tips.

headlines.title: Amazon’s secret sale on air conditioners and fans can help you beat the heat
headlines.description: Amazon has hidden deals on window air conditioners, portable AC units and personal fans from Shark, LG and more, with prices up to 35% off.

headlines.title: Beef tallow as moisturizer? I asked derms if this trend is genius or gross
headlines.description: Is beef tallow a good moisturizer? Dermatologists break down the benefits, risks, and whether this viral skincare trend is worth trying.

headlines.title: U.S. military will test service members’ testosterone levels, Pete Hegseth says
headlines.description: Hegseth said service members ages 30 and older will undergo screenings to ensure they “have the biological foundation required to sustain the fight.”

headlines.title: Nearly 7,000 cases of cyclosporiasis confirmed or under investigation nationwide, CDC says
headlines.description: Michigan health officials suggested lettuce or salad greens could be possible sources. Taco Bell said it had removed some ingredients “as a precautionary measure.”

headlines.title: Lettuce or salad greens could be source of diarrhea-causing illness, Michigan health officials say
headlines.description: The foodborne gastrointestinal illness has shown up in 31 states, and federal health officials say 86 people have been hospitalized.

headlines.title: Dozens hospitalized with cyclosporiasis as cases of gastrointestinal illness spike in 31 states
headlines.description: Nearly 3,000 people have been sickened with a gastrointestinal illness called cyclosporiasis, a foodborne infection marked by intense, watery diarrhea, state health officials report.
